what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG icon showing two overlapping rounded checklist sheets, with a check mark on the foreground sheet.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with no explicit width or height.
The root sets fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width="1.5", stroke-linecap="round", and stroke-linejoin="round".
Two rounded rectangles form overlapping sheets at x/y positions 5/3 and 3/5.
A path draws a check mark using relative line commands.
standouts: The icon combines a stacked-document shape with a checklist check mark.
The stroke inherits currentColor while the front sheet uses a fixed white fill.
conventions: Uses a compact inline SVG icon structure.
Follows common outline-icon conventions with currentColor strokes, rounded caps, and rounded joins.
Uses child-level fill override for the foreground rectangle.
concerns: The hard-coded white fill may need consumer handling on non-white surfaces.
No title or desc is included, so non-decorative accessibility text would need to come from usage context.
notes: A compact outline SVG for a double checklist or stacked task-list document icon.
questions: Should the foreground fill be theme-driven or intentionally fixed to white?
Is this icon intended to be decorative-only, or should consumers provide an accessible label?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a double checklist icon, and the SVG depicts overlapping checklist sheets with a check mark.
apparent_themes: theme-colored stroke with fixed fill accent
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a simple reusable checklist/double-document icon that inherits surrounding text color while preserving a white foreground sheet.
novelty: The fixed white foreground rectangle contrasts with the currentColor outline treatment.
contribution: Contributes a compact stacked-checklist visual substrate with currentColor outline styling and a filled foreground page treatment.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 SVG outline icon of two overlapping rounded checklist sheets with a single check mark on the foreground sheet.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing checklist, task completion, or layered list/document concepts in an icon set.
